rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Use component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings such as assignments when they are valid in that scope
Do not use subcomponent ports in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in the intended target component or implementation while preserving the AADL architecture
Use visible identifiers from the current visible identifiers list when available
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees inside annex agree only when the case supplies concrete bounds, named constants, or a visible relation
Write output dependency contracts that relate visible outputs to visible inputs in component type annexes
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope; otherwise omit the unsafe clause
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for the properties
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers
Do not encode AADL property names as AGREE variables
